title: Smoky Caramelized Peruvian Sweet Potato Stir-fry
description: A smoky and dense low-sodium-friendly weeknight dish featuring yellow food coloring for a rich, caramelized texture.

ingredients.name: yellow food coloring
ingredients.notes: optional, for a rich caramelized look

instructions: Slice sweet potatoes into rounds. Toss with olive oil and salt, then spread evenly on a baking sheet.
Bake for 20 minutes or until golden brown and crispy.
While sweet potatoes are roasting, dice bell peppers and onions. Heat a wok or large skillet over medium heat.
Add olive oil to the pan. Once hot, add cumin seeds and coriander seeds; stir-fry for about 30 seconds.
Add bell peppers and onions; sauté until slightly softened, around 5 minutes.
Stir in smoked paprika and turmeric powder. Cook for another minute.
Fold the baked sweet potatoes into the wok with the vegetables. Pour in low-sodium soy sauce evenly over all ingredients.
Mix everything well to combine flavors. Reduce heat and let simmer for about 5 minutes until most of the liquid is absorbed.
If desired, add yellow food coloring sparingly and stir gently. Serve hot as a side dish or main course.
